scope.summary: Implement coordinator routing policies and explicit advisory-to-authority promotion.
forbidden_paths: automatic code/contract promotion without explicit policy
npm/JavaScript proof targets for this WP
app/src-tauri launch or IPC authority for this WP
proof commands that pass with zero matching tests
acceptance_criteria: Routing policies cover local-first, cloud-review, cloud-plan-local-execute, parallel-debate, validator lane, and operator lane.
Advisory output cannot modify authority until promoted through a typed decision.
Disagreement produces explicit messages and a coordinator decision artifact.

Promotion MUST be modeled as an explicit deterministic state machine: advisory -> promotion_requested -> pending_policy|pending_approval -> approved|denied|expired -> executing -> executed|skipped|unsupported, keyed by stable action/request ids and idempotency keys. Only PromotionGate resolution records may mutate authority.

PromotionGate MUST deny CRDT/model-lane promotions when base_snapshot_ref, state_vector, schema_id, or expected EventLedger aggregate/version is stale or mismatched. The denial MUST be typed, durable, replayable, and include the current state_vector/base snapshot or recovery hint; stale proposals MUST NOT auto-rebase into authority.

Routing and promotion decisions persist as EventLedger-backed decision artifacts with idempotency key, selected input refs, rejected input refs, validator/operator authority, and trace/span correlation.
Disagreement and stalled progress produce bounded state transitions and explicit recovery/next-action records instead of recursive agent chatter.

ModelLane promotion input MUST include stable sorted input refs, canonical hash basis, selected/rejected refs, validator/operator authority identity, expected EventLedger aggregate/version, base_snapshot_ref, state_vector, schema_id, deterministic tie-break rule, and decision identity.

Negative proof MUST cover duplicate idempotency, stale base, mismatched schema, mismatched aggregate version, direct authority mutation, and reordered input refs producing the same deterministic decision.

After implementation and before READY_FOR_VALIDATION, this MT MUST receive post-implementation adversarial review against the actual diff. Findings must be fixed or converted into explicit lifecycle blocker/missing_resource/proof_status state; the implementer may not self-certify COMPLETED.
scope.risk_if_missed: A swarm can look parallel while still depending on hidden prompt arbitration.

implementation_notes: Implement routing policies as typed Rust enums/structs, not stringly prompt conventions.
Required policies: local_first, cloud_review, cloud_plan_local_execute, parallel_debate, validator_lane, operator_lane.
Every cross-lane message must use typed routing fields; narrative-only handoff is forbidden.
Advisory lane output must not mutate code, contracts, validation verdicts, or authority state until PromotionGate/operator/validator promotion records it.
Disagreement must create typed critique/vote/decision messages plus a selected decision artifact; downstream lanes receive the selected decision through ContextBundle.
Provider feature gaps must fail closed with explicit CX-MM or provider capability error codes; no silent fallback.
Temporal-style durable replay maps to deterministic routing records plus recorded non-deterministic adapter calls; rerun/replay must not depend on live provider output.
Local-first suggesting/pull-request patterns map to model proposals that Handshake accepts/rejects/promotes, not to direct model writes.
Locus must be updated with run/lane progress and MT association whenever coordinator state changes a governed work unit.
Use Flight Recorder/EventLedger payloads as bounded events plus artifact refs; do not put unbounded model output, raw secrets, or prompt-only state in event payloads.

If this MT changes any product behavior, storage/event contract, command, IPC/API path, diagnostic surface, model lane, or navigation path, update the in-product UserManual in the same implementation change per HBR-MAN-004.

Product implementation for this MT must be Rust-only: Rust backend plus Rust native egui/Argus surfaces where UI is touched. Legacy app/src, app/src-tauri, React, Tauri/WebView, TypeScript, JavaScript, docs/model-manual, and npm proof targets are reference-only or forbidden and MUST NOT satisfy implementation/proof authority.

Proof commands must name exact Rust test targets or exact test names. Zero matching tests is a hard failure. Deterministic provider fakes are allowed for cloud/local model calls, but PostgreSQL/EventLedger/ArtifactStore/CRDT/UserManual/Argus authority must touch real Handshake-owned Rust resources or an explicit real PostgreSQL URL.
Each behavior-changing MT must include same-change UserManual coverage for the changed behavior; MT-011 audits coverage and cannot substitute for same-change HBR-MAN-004 proof.

This pre-implementation contract review does not replace post-implementation review. After coding, run adversarial review against the actual diff through the listed lenses and update MT status signaling before handoff.

risks: Consensus can be implied by last-writer-wins prompts.
minimum_controls: Promotion state must be part of a ModelLaneMessage or linked decision record.

research_basis.authority_posture: Master spec/SPEC_CURRENT/product code are authority; HANDSHAKE_PRODUCT_REFERENCE.md and external research are navigation/evidence only.
patterns_applied: Typed multi-agent orchestration becomes ModelLaneRun/ModelLane/ModelLaneMessage records, never peer-to-peer chat authority.
Durable workflow/replay patterns become PostgreSQL/EventLedger aggregates, checkpoints, projections, and deterministic state transitions.
CRDT research becomes explicit draft/proposal/update/state-vector/snapshot/stale-base records before promotion.
Trace/span research becomes propagated trace_id/span_id/model_session_id/lane_id correlation across lanes, tools, providers, artifacts, and diagnostics.
Tool protocol research becomes Handshake capability check, consent/projection, Flight Recorder/EventLedger logging, execution, result capture, and recovery.
rejected_options: peer-to-peer model channels outside coordinator-owned ModelLaneMessage records
legacy app/src React/Tauri code as runtime authority
SQLite runtime/test/fixture/cache/fallback authority for this WP
Flight Recorder-only replay without PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority rows
manual or markdown prose as product runtime authority
research_basis.selected_handshake_translation: Coordinator routing and promotion must be a deterministic Rust state machine with idempotency keys, stale-base denial, evidence refs, and PromotionGate/ValidationRunner authority boundaries.

handshake_compatibility.product_reference_file_posture: reference/navigation only; do not override SPEC_CURRENT, master spec, build rules, or product code evidence
handshake_compatibility.proposal_promotion_model: Models may propose, critique, summarize, and produce artifacts; Handshake validates/promotes deterministically through Rust backend authority surfaces before state becomes authoritative.
durable_authority: CRDT/write-box when shared editable state is touched
observability_required: HBR-INT-009 tier posture for Flight Recorder, internal_diagnostics, Palmistry
handshake_compatibility.no_context_model_requirement: A model with no chat history must be able to implement and verify this MT from this contract, SPEC_CURRENT/master spec, product code paths, UserManual/backend navigation, and listed proof targets.
handshake_compatibility.rust_only_implementation: Rust backend plus Rust native egui/Argus only. TypeScript/React/app/src/app-src-tauri/npm/docs-model-manual are not implementation or proof authority for this WP.
handshake_compatibility.spec_realism_gate: Implementer may move only to READY_FOR_VALIDATION after exact Rust proof targets touch the named real resources; COMPLETED is validator-only.
